document: circular on the regulatory framework on open banking in nigeria (Nigeria - Row 8)

sections.heading: Risk Management……………………………………………….14
sections.title: Risk management framework for open banking participants
sections.summary: This section establishes risk management requirements for open banking participants in Nigeria. It defines risk maturity levels (Tiers 0-3) based on regulatory licensing, specifies data and service access categories with associated risk ratings (PIST: Low, MIT: Moderate, PIFT: High, PAST: High & Sensitive), and mandates data access governance through tiered registration requirements. Participants must implement robust risk assessment procedures, maintain API risk catalogs, and establish monitoring systems for partner participants to ensure compliance with the framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires data transfer impact assessments to evaluate risk maturity levels, data category risk ratings, and access governance mechanisms for cross-border data sharing in open banking. It mandates specific technical and operational safeguards for high-risk data categories like PIFT and PAST.

requirements.text: Participants must define risk maturity levels (Tiers 0-3) based on regulatory licensing
Tier 0 participants require on-boarding by sponsoring Tier 2 or Tier 3 participants
Sponsoring participants must register Tier 0 participants on Open Banking Registry within 3 working days
Tier 1 participants must be admitted to CBN regulatory sandbox
Tier 2 participants must hold valid CBN license and undergo risk assessment by two partner participants
Tier 3 participants must hold valid CBN license and undergo risk assessment by two partner participants
All participants must implement API risk catalogs and process control mapping
Providers must publish APIs and define technical guidelines
Providers must carry out Know Your Partner (KYP) due diligence on partner participants
Providers must ensure partner participants obtain end-user consent based on agreed protocols
Providers must share responsibility for end-user losses not arising from wilful negligence
API Users must obtain end-user consent for each action on their account
All participants must maintain customer service desks operating 24/7 for complaint resolution
All participants must comply with data privacy laws and regulations

sections.source_quote: The Central Bank of Nigeria shall regulate the development of a common Banking Industry API standard with technical design standard, data standard, information security standard and operational rules.
sections.relevance_reason: Risk management for data transfers and financial data handling
